codeReview.summary: Pass. The feature does exactly what the ticket asked for: `index.js:6` imports `src/lib/polyfills` before app code, `src/lib/polyfills.ts:17-42` adds a minimal idempotent `AbortSignal.timeout` shim that only installs when the runtime lacks the WHATWG static factory, and `src/lib/__tests__/polyfills.test.ts:46-112` covers presence, abort behavior, the Hermes-like missing-native path, idempotent reloads, and a basic non-regression check for the existing text-encoding polyfill. I found no correctness issues in the implementation itself.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the mission/library reads that the `mobile-feature-worker` honesty gate requires before claiming compliance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:12-29` requires reading `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `validation-contract.md`, and relevant library docs (at minimum `architecture.md`, plus `environment.md` for install/test flows). `worker-transcripts.jsonl:83` shows this session reading `src/lib/polyfills.ts`, `.factory/services.yaml`, `.factory/library/ci-debug-emulator-pkarr-put-undefined-is-not-a-function.md`, `index.js`, `jest.setup.js`, `jest.config.js`, and `package.json`, but not those required mission/library files. `handoffs/2026-04-23T23-50-32-681Z__polyfill-abortsignal-timeout__a614bea2-2101-441b-a752-19662cd2941d.json:74-77` records `"followedProcedure": true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The same handoff claims the procedure was followed even though the transcript shows implementation edits landing before the red-first test step the skill mandates for non-trivial behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:29-32` and `:66-73` require failing tests first and an observed red phase before implementation. In `worker-transcripts.jsonl:83`, the worker performs two `Edit` calls against `src/lib/polyfills.ts` before creating `src/lib/__tests__/polyfills.test.ts` and before the first targeted Jest run (`bun run test -- --testPathPattern src/lib/__tests__/polyfills.test.ts`). `handoffs/2026-04-23T23-50-32-681Z__polyfill-abortsignal-timeout__a614bea2-2101-441b-a752-19662cd2941d.json:74-77` still reports `"followedProcedure": true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is a useful test-harness quirk around `src/lib/polyfills.ts` that is not captured in shared docs: its `wrapSubtleMethod(...)` diagnostics mutate Node's built-in `crypto.subtle` under Jest and can make an otherwise-green run exit non-zero unless tests mask `crypto.subtle` while loading the module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The worker had to document and work around this in the handoff: `handoffs/2026-04-23T23-50-32-681Z__polyfill-abortsignal-timeout__a614bea2-2101-441b-a752-19662cd2941d.json:11` says the new test hides `globalThis.crypto.subtle` during module load, and `:67-72` records the underlying non-blocking issue plus a suggested follow-up. I did not find the same Jest/polyfills caveat documented under `.factory/library/`.

summary: Pass on the code: the shim is in the right place, is guarded/idempotent, and the added tests exercise the intended behavior. I recorded three shared-state observations: the worker claimed skill-procedure compliance without transcript evidence of the required context reads, without a red-first sequence, and the Jest `crypto.subtle`/polyfills quirk discovered here is not yet captured in shared mission knowledge.
